data.title: Tesla did not increase its Bitcoin holdings in the first quarter and recorded a $173 million asset loss.
data.description: Tesla disclosed that it did not adjust its Bitcoin holdings in the first quarter of 2026, still holding 11,509 BTC, worth approximately $880 million at current prices. Due to the drop in Bitcoin's price from approximately $90,000 to $68,000, the company recorded an after-tax fair value loss of approximately $173 million on its digital assets. Despite this, Tesla's overall financial performance was mixed, with earnings exceeding expectations but revenue slightly below market expectations, driving its stock price up approximately 4% in after-hours trading.

data.title: White House: Trump has not set a deadline for a ceasefire with Iran.
data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt stated that the final decision on extending the ceasefire agreement with Iran will rest with President Trump. "The president has not set a specific deadline, and I've seen that mentioned in some reports today. I know there are some anonymous sources reporting a three- to five-day grace period. That's not true," she added, noting that Trump is awaiting a "unified response" from Iranian leaders regarding the terms of the US peace agreement.

data.title: TD Cowen: Crypto legislation faces obstacles, not just due to controversy over stablecoin yields.
data.description: Investment bank TD Cowen stated that the disagreements surrounding the CLARITY Act extend far beyond stablecoin yields, with multiple practical obstacles potentially slowing the legislative process. First, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission is understaffed, currently holding only one commissioner. Under these circumstances, Congress is hesitant to entrust the agency with more crypto regulatory responsibilities, and filling vacancies alone will take months. Second, the issue of prediction markets is escalating. Whether to include them in the bill's regulation, and potential insider trading and political conflicts of interest (including controversies related to Trump-related projects), could cause some Democratic lawmakers to oppose the bill. Meanwhile, the ongoing controversy surrounding the Trump family's crypto project, World Liberty Financial, is increasing the bill's political sensitivity, making bipartisan consensus more difficult to achieve. Geopolitics is also a variable. Discussions about Iran potentially using crypto payments are intensifying focus on anti-money laundering provisions and could even introduce amendments detrimental to the industry. Furthermore, some lawmakers are attempting to include the Credit Card Competition Act, which, if pursued, could trigger new conflicts of interest, further hindering the overall legislation.

data.title: UK Government Advances Stablecoin Regulations to Boost Fintech Sector
data.description: The UK government has announced new regulations for stablecoins and tokenized payments as part of its strategy to enhance the fintech sector. According to NS3.AI, the Treasury highlighted that sterling-backed stablecoins currently have a market value of approximately $30 million, based on data from DefiLlama. In a move to streamline processes for stablecoin issuers, the Treasury has appointed Chris Woolard as the wholesale digital markets champion and is set to introduce legislation aimed at reducing administrative burdens. The Financial Conduct Authority's framework for cryptoassets, which includes stablecoins, is scheduled to be implemented in October 2027.

data.title: PUSD to Launch on ADI Chain Targeting Islamic Finance Market
data.description: PUSD is preparing to launch on the ADI Chain, aiming to capture a share of the Islamic finance market, which exceeds $3 trillion. According to NS3.AI, the stablecoin is designed to comply with Shariah law and currently has approximately $2.3 billion in circulation. It is backed on a 1:1 basis by reserves held in Saudi riyals and UAE dirhams.

data.title: Prediction Market Platform Seeks to Demonstrate Strong Controls
data.description: A prediction market platform embroiled in a regulatory conflict between federal and state authorities is aiming to showcase robust controls, according to CoinDesk. The platform's efforts come amid ongoing scrutiny as it navigates the complex regulatory landscape. This move is part of its strategy to address compliance challenges and reinforce its operational integrity.

data.title: Bitcoin Dominance Surpasses 60% for First Time This Year
data.description: Bitcoin's market dominance has exceeded 60% for the first time this year. According to NS3.AI, this metric reflects Bitcoin's share of the total cryptocurrency market capitalization, indicating a significant shift in market dynamics.

data.title: Kalshi Penalizes Political Candidates for Betting on Elections
data.description: Kalshi has imposed a $6,300 fine on Mark Moran and banned three political candidates from participating in its platform for five years due to their involvement in betting on their own elections. According to NS3.AI, Matt Klein and Ezekiel Enriquez have also agreed to settlements of $540 and $784, respectively. Kalshi stated that Moran faced a more severe penalty. This disclosure comes amid increased scrutiny from Democratic lawmakers concerning the risks of insider trading in prediction markets, affecting platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.

data.title: Xiaomi Unveils MiMo-V2.5 and MiMo-V2.5-Pro with Enhanced Multimedia Features
data.description: Xiaomi has introduced its latest models, MiMo-V2.5 and MiMo-V2.5-Pro, which boast advanced image, audio, and video capabilities along with a 1M-token context window. According to NS3.AI, Xiaomi announced that MiMo-V2.5-Pro achieved a score of 57.2% on the SWE-bench Pro. The company plans to open-source these models soon, aiming to enhance accessibility and innovation in multimedia technology.

data.title: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust Achieves Record Holdings Amid Inflows
data.description: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) has reached a new milestone, holding 806,700 BTC valued at approximately $63.7 billion. According to NS3.AI, this achievement comes after nine consecutive trading days of net inflows, positioning IBIT with around 49% of U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF assets. Over the past four weeks, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have seen positive net inflows totaling about $2 billion, with IBIT contributing approximately $1.7 billion to this figure.

data.title: Company Holds 11,509 BTC Valued at $880 Million
data.description: The company continues to hold 11,509 BTC, valued at approximately $880 million based on bitcoin's current price of around $78,000, according to CoinDesk. This unchanged bitcoin stash reflects the company's ongoing strategy in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Tesla Reports Unchanged Bitcoin Holdings Amid Digital Asset Impairment Loss
data.description: Tesla has maintained its Bitcoin holdings at 11,509 BTC during the first quarter of 2026, despite booking a $173 million after-tax impairment loss on its digital assets. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin's value decreased from approximately $90,000 at the beginning of the year to around $68,000 by the end of March. In response to these developments, Tesla's stock saw a 4% increase in after-hours trading.

data.title: STOCKS | IBM Shares Drop 8% in After-Hours Trading
data.description: IBM's stock experienced a significant decline, falling 8% in after-hours trading. According to Jin10, the drop comes amid market reactions to recent developments concerning the company. Investors are closely monitoring the situation to assess potential impacts on IBM's future performance.

data.title: New York Governor Hochul Restricts State Employees from Using Insider Information in Prediction Markets
data.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul has signed an executive order prohibiting certain state employees and governor-appointed public authority members from leveraging nonpublic information for profit in prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, this decision follows a similar directive issued by Illinois Governor JB Pritzker a day earlier, targeting state employees. Kalshi, a prediction market platform, announced on Wednesday that it has initiated three insider trading cases involving candidates, resulting in fines and suspensions for betting on their own races.

data.title: Tesla Reports Q1 2026 Revenue of $22.387 Billion
data.description: Tesla has announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, revealing a revenue of $22.387 billion. According to Jin10, this marks an increase from the $19.335 billion recorded in the same period last year. However, the revenue fell short of market expectations, which had anticipated $22.792 billion for the quarter.

data.title: Tesla Shares Rise 3% in After-Hours Trading
data.description: Tesla's stock experienced a 3% increase in after-hours trading. According to Jin10, this movement follows recent developments in the company's performance and market conditions. Investors are closely monitoring Tesla's trajectory as it continues to navigate the evolving automotive and energy sectors.

data.title: Strategy's STRC Preferred Equity Funds Major Bitcoin Purchase
data.description: Strategy's STRC preferred equity accounted for approximately 85% of the financing for its recent acquisition of 34,164 Bitcoin, valued at around $2.5 billion. According to NS3.AI, the security offers an 11.5% dividend, attracting significant interest from investors focused on yield. However, Dom Kwok cautioned that Strategy retains the right to suspend dividends at any time. Satish Patel noted that favorable tax treatment and reduced volatility have bolstered demand for this investment.

data.title: Flying Tulip Introduces Rate-Limiting Module for DeFi Lending
data.description: Flying Tulip has unveiled a new programmatic module designed to manage DeFi lending outflows during periods of abnormal withdrawals. According to NS3.AI, the system aims to address issues related to smart contract exploits, oracle failures, and significant position unwinds. Andre Cronje, a key figure in the development, highlighted that the module includes a fail-open mechanism to prevent the locking of funds, ensuring smoother operations during unforeseen events.

data.title: U.S. Indo-Pacific Command Engages in Bitcoin Node Testing for Cybersecurity
data.description: Admiral Samuel Paparo has revealed that the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command is actively running a Bitcoin node as part of its cybersecurity research efforts. According to NS3.AI, Paparo informed the Senate Armed Services Committee on April 21 that the proof-of-work mechanism inherent in Bitcoin has significant applications in computer science, particularly for safeguarding data and increasing the operational costs for cyber adversaries. This marks the first instance of a sitting U.S. combatant commander publicly discussing Bitcoin as a national security asset during congressional hearings. As of April 22, the Department of Defense had not provided further details on the scope of these operational tests.

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Index Gains 1% as U.S. Stocks Rise Late in the Session
data.description: U.S. stocks experienced a late surge, with the S&P 500 Index expanding its gains to 1%. According to Jin10, this upward movement in the market reflects a positive sentiment among investors, contributing to the broader rally in the stock market. The late-session rise indicates increased confidence in the economic outlook, as investors continue to monitor key financial indicators and corporate earnings reports. This development comes amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy and economic growth prospects.
